opts: (A) A programming concept where a function calls itself
(B) A data structure where a node points to its parent
(C) A control structure where a loop continues indefinitely
(D) A debugging tool where errors are reported
questions.explanation: Recursion is a programming concept where a function calls itself until it reaches a base case, which stops the recursion.

questions.q: What is the base case in recursion?
opts: (A) The condition that stops the recursion
(B) The condition that starts the recursion
(C) The condition that repeats the recursion
(D) The condition that skips the recursion
questions.explanation: The base case is the condition that stops the recursion, preventing infinite loops.

questions.q: What is the time complexity of a recursive function?
opts: (A) O(1) - constant time
(B) O(n) - linear time
(C) O(2^n) - exponential time
(D) O(n!) - factorial time
questions.explanation: The time complexity of a recursive function can be exponential, depending on the number of recursive calls.
questions.topic: Time and Space Complexity of Recursion

opts: (A) A technique to store intermediate results
(B) A technique to skip intermediate results
(C) A technique to repeat the same calculation
(D) A technique to skip the base case
questions.explanation: Memoization is a technique to store intermediate results in a recursive function, improving performance.

questions.q: What is the difference between recursion and iteration?
opts: (A) Recursion uses loops, iteration uses recursion
(B) Recursion uses function calls, iteration uses loops
(C) Recursion uses recursive function calls, iteration uses iterative loops
(D) Recursion uses iterative loops, iteration uses recursive function calls
questions.explanation: Recursion uses recursive function calls, while iteration uses iterative loops.

questions.q: What is the maximum depth of a recursive call stack?
opts: (B) n
(D) Any value
questions.explanation: The maximum depth of a recursive call stack is equal to the number of recursive calls.
questions.topic: Time and Space Complexity of Recursion

questions.q: What is the space complexity of a recursive function?
opts: (A) O(1) - constant space
(B) O(n) - linear space
(C) O(2^n) - exponential space
(D) O(n!) - factorial space
questions.explanation: The space complexity of a recursive function can be exponential, depending on the number of recursive calls.
questions.topic: Time and Space Complexity of Recursion

questions.q: How can we prevent infinite recursion?
opts: (A) By using a base case
(B) By using a recursive function call
(C) By using a loop
(D) By using a different data structure
questions.explanation: A base case is necessary to prevent infinite recursion, as it stops the recursive calls.

questions.q: What is the problem with recursive functions when dealing with large datasets?
opts: (A) They are too slow
(B) They use too much memory
(C) They can cause a stack overflow
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Recursive functions can be slow and use too much memory, and they can also cause a stack overflow for large datasets.
questions.topic: Time and Space Complexity of Recursion

questions.q: How can we optimize a recursive function for large datasets?
opts: (A) By using memoization
(B) By using dynamic programming
(C) By using a different algorithm
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Memoization, dynamic programming, and using a different algorithm are all ways to optimize a recursive function for large datasets.

questions.q: What is the difference between a recursive function and a recursive algorithm?
opts: (A) A recursive function is a type of recursive algorithm
(B) A recursive algorithm is a type of recursive function
(C) A recursive function is a single function call, while a recursive algorithm is multiple function calls
(D) A recursive function is a loop, while a recursive algorithm is a function call
questions.explanation: A recursive function is a single function call, while a recursive algorithm is multiple function calls.

questions.q: What is the concept of tail recursion?
opts: (A) A recursive function that calls itself as the last operation
(B) A recursive function that calls itself as the first operation
(C) A recursive function that uses a loop
(D) A recursive function that skips the base case
questions.explanation: Tail recursion is a recursive function that calls itself as the last operation, which can be optimized by the compiler.

questions.q: What is the problem with using recursion for sorting large datasets?
opts: (A) It is too slow
(B) It uses too much memory
(C) It can cause a stack overflow
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Recursive functions for sorting large datasets can be slow, use too much memory, and cause a stack overflow.
questions.topic: Time and Space Complexity of Recursion

questions.q: How can we convert a recursive function to an iterative one?
opts: (A) By using a loop instead of a function call
(B) By using a recursive function call instead of a loop
(C) By using a different algorithm
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Converting a recursive function to an iterative one typically involves replacing the function calls with a loop.

questions.q: What is the concept of recursive backtracking?
opts: (A) A technique to explore all possible solutions
(B) A technique to skip possible solutions
(C) A technique to repeat the same solution
(D) A technique to skip the base case
questions.explanation: Recursive backtracking is a technique to explore all possible solutions by recursively trying all possibilities.

questions.q: What is the problem with using recursion for solving dynamic programming problems?
opts: (A) It is too slow
(B) It uses too much memory
(C) It can cause a stack overflow
(D) It does not use memoization
questions.explanation: Recursive functions for dynamic programming problems should use memoization to avoid redundant calculations.

questions.q: How can we use recursion to solve the Tower of Hanoi problem?
opts: (A) By using a recursive function to move the disks one by one
(B) By using a recursive function to move all disks at once
(C) By using a loop to move the disks
(D) By using a different algorithm
questions.explanation: Recursion can be used to solve the Tower of Hanoi problem by recursively moving the disks one by one.
